title: DNA extraction and qPCR for Trypanosoma cruzi
author: Brooke E. White

steps.title: Take 3-10 mL blood freshly collected...
steps.instruction: Take 3-10 mL blood freshly collected in K 2 EDTA (purple top) blood collection tubes preserved on ice and centrifuge at 2000 g for 15 minutes to separate plasma from blood cells. Note: If blood is previously separated and frozen, thaw completely before starting extraction.

steps.title: Transfer plasma layer to storage tube(s)...
steps.instruction: Transfer plasma layer to storage tube(s) with pre-printed labels detailing sample information and date and store at -80 for use as needed.

steps.title: DNA Extraction Part 1 (Lysis )
steps.instruction: DNA Extraction Part 1 (Lysis )

steps.title: Transfer the cell pellet from blood...
steps.instruction: Transfer the cell pellet from blood into a nuclease-free 50 mL conical tube and bring the volume up to 10mL with PBS Note: You can use plasma, whole blood, or the cell pellet here but we have found the cell pellet to be the most sensitive.

steps.title: Add 250 μL Proteinase K solution (included with kit)
steps.instruction: Add 250 μL Proteinase K solution (included with kit)

steps.title: Vortex to mix well. Note
steps.instruction: Vortex to mix well. Note: Tubes should be tightly closed before vortexing.

steps.title: Add 10
steps.instruction: Add 10.2 mL BL Buffer (included with kit).

steps.title: Add 20 μL RNase A (Included with kit)
steps.instruction: Add 20 μL RNase A (Included with kit).

steps.title: Vortex to mix thoroughly
steps.instruction: Vortex to mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Incubate at 70 °C in Shake-N-Bake...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 70 °C in Shake-N-Bake incubator for 30 minutes, vortexing the tube once at the halfway point. Note: Shaker platform should be on medium rocking speed during incubation.

steps.title: DNA Extraction Part 2 (DNA isolation)
steps.instruction: DNA Extraction Part 2 (DNA isolation)

steps.title: Insert a column into a 50mL...
steps.instruction: Insert a column into a 50mL collection tube, both from the kit.

steps.title: Add 10
steps.instruction: Add 10.3 mL 100% ethanol to the lysed blood mixture and vortex to mix.

steps.title: Transfer the lysed sample to the column, including any precipitates
steps.instruction: Transfer the lysed sample to the column, including any precipitates.

steps.title: Centrifuge column and tube at 4,000 rcf for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge column and tube at 4,000 rcf for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Repeat Steps 4
steps.instruction: Repeat Steps 4.3 + 4.4 until entire sample is transferred. Each transfer will be approximately 15 mL.

steps.title: Discard the filtrate in the tube, retaining the collection tube
steps.instruction: Discard the filtrate in the tube, retaining the collection tube.

steps.title: Add 5 mL HBC Buffer (included...
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL HBC Buffer (included with kit) to the column. Note: HBC Buffer must be diluted w/ isopropanol before use.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4,000 rcf for 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4,000 rcf for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Discard the filtrate and retain the collection tube
steps.instruction: Discard the filtrate and retain the collection tube.

steps.title: Add 15 mL DNA Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 15 mL DNA Wash Buffer (included with kit) to the column. Note: Wash Buffer must be diluted w/ethanol before use.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4,000 rcf for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4,000 rcf for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Discard filtrate and reuse the collection tube
steps.instruction: Discard filtrate and reuse the collection tube.

steps.title: Add 15 mL DNA Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 15 mL DNA Wash Buffer for a second wash step.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4,000 rcf for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4,000 rcf for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Discard filtrate and reuse the collection tube
steps.instruction: Discard filtrate and reuse the collection tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge the empty column at 4,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the empty column at 4,000 rcf for 10 minutes to dry the column. Note: This is critical to remove any trace ethanol that could interfere with downstream applications.

steps.title: Transfer the column into a new...
steps.instruction: Transfer the column into a new nuclease-free 50 mL tube Note: Conical tube not provided with kit

steps.title: Dry the column further at 70...
steps.instruction: Dry the column further at 70 °C for 10 minutes inside the Shake-N-Bake incubator.

steps.title: Add 500 μL HOT (70°C) Elution...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL HOT (70°C) Elution Buffer (included with kit) directly to the filter inside the column.

steps.title: Let sit at room temperature for 3-5 minutes
steps.instruction: Let sit at room temperature for 3-5 minutes.

steps.title: Wipe down the inside of the...
steps.instruction: Wipe down the inside of the centrifuge with bleach followed by water to prevent corrosion. Buckets and bucket inserts should also be washed thoroughly during this time. Note: This step is important for contamination prevention.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4,000 rcf for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4,000 rcf for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Transfer eluted volume back onto the...
steps.instruction: Transfer eluted volume back onto the filter inside the column and add 150 μL fresh Elution Buffer.

steps.title: Let sit at room temperature for 3-5 minutes
steps.instruction: Let sit at room temperature for 3-5 minutes.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4,000 rcf for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4,000 rcf for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Transfer eluted material into nuclease-free 1
steps.instruction: Transfer eluted material into nuclease-free 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Measure DNA quantity
steps.instruction: Measure DNA quantity.

steps.title: Store eluted DNA at -20°C (or...
steps.instruction: Store eluted DNA at -20°C (or 4°C if planning PCR soon).

steps.title: After fragmenting, dilute DNA to desired...
steps.instruction: After fragmenting, dilute DNA to desired concentration (200 ng/µl) in ultrapure water. Preferably in 96 well plates to make transfer to qpcr plate easier.

steps.title: Recipe for master mix for each...
steps.instruction: Recipe for master mix for each pcr plate A B C 1 Component Volume per 20µl reaction Volume per 96 well plate (106 reactions) 2 premix itaq univeral probe mix 10 1060 3 Cruzi 1 primer (10 µM) 1.5 159 4 Cruzi 2 primer (10 µM) 1.5 159 5 IAC primer FW (10 µM) 0.2 21.2 6 IAC primer Rev (10 µM) 0.2 21.2 7 Cruzi 3 probe 0.1 10.6 8 IAC probe 0.1 10.6 9 Water 0.4 42.4

steps.title: Add 14 µl mastermix + 1...
steps.instruction: Add 14 µl mastermix + 1 µl IAC (25 pg/ µl) + 5 µl diluted sample DNA to each well

steps.title: Follow cycling conditions listed below
steps.instruction: Follow cycling conditions listed below. Be sure to select "all channels" when running protocol

steps.title: A B 1 PCR temperature time...
steps.instruction: A B 1 PCR temperature time 2 95 3 min 3 95 15 sec 4 58 1 min 5 repeat steps 2 and 3 x50 conditions for Bio Rad Opus 384

steps.title: Export program file at the end...
steps.instruction: Export program file at the end of protocol and analyze using CFX Manager software

steps.title: A B 1 Component sequence 2...
steps.instruction: A B 1 Component sequence 2 Cruzi 1 ASTCGGCTGATCGTTTTCGA 3 Cruzi 2 AATTCCTCCAAGCAGCGGATA 4 IAC F ACCGTCATGGAACAGCACGTA 5 IAC R CTCCCGCAACAAACCCTATAAAT 6 Cruzi3 probe 5'-Fam-CACACACTGGACACCAA-NFQ-MGB-3' 7 IAC probe 5’ -VIC-AGCATCTGTTCTTGAAGGT-NFQ-MGB-3’ Primer and probe details

steps.title: A B 1 IAC (Internal Amplification...
steps.instruction: A B 1 IAC (Internal Amplification control) (g-block from IDT) TGTTCTTAAAGTAACCGTCATGGAACAGCACGTACCGATTTATAAGATTGCTGGAGAAATGACTGGATTTGGAGCATCTGTTCTTGAAGGTGTTTTAGCTTTCGTCTTGGTTTATACTGTGTTCACGGCTAGCGATCCCAGACGTGGGCTACCTTTAGCAGTGGGACCTATATTTATAGGGTTTGTTGCGGGAGCCAATGTTCTAGCCGC IAC details

steps.title: Wrap 1
steps.instruction: Wrap 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes containing extracted DNA in parafilm

steps.title: Place samples in floating foam rack
steps.instruction: Place samples in floating foam rack

steps.title: Place in chilled cuphorn
steps.instruction: Place in chilled cuphorn

steps.title: Set sonifier to continuous mode with...
steps.instruction: Set sonifier to continuous mode with a time interval of 50 seconds and 60% amplitude

steps.title: Start sonifier and repeat sonication 5...
steps.instruction: Start sonifier and repeat sonication 5 times for a total of 5 cycles of 50 seconds with 60% amplitude

steps.title: Place samples back on ice and continue preparations for qPCR
steps.instruction: Place samples back on ice and continue preparations for qPCR

steps.title: Include no template controls (NTC) and...
steps.instruction: Include no template controls (NTC) and a sample with known T.cruzi DNA concentration in each plate. Note: For our typical assay, we run samples in 384 well plates and include 16 NTC reactions in each plate.

steps.title: Spin down briefly before beginning cycler program
steps.instruction: Spin down briefly before beginning cycler program.
